summary: Completed the C match for MSL __strtoul, allowing asm/MSL/strtoul.s to be deleted and removed from obj_files.mk. The replacement implements the base/max-width scanner as a state machine with ReadProc/fetch/unfetch callbacks, handling whitespace, signs, base autodetection including 0x, digit conversion, chars_scanned, negative, and overflow. The PR also cleaned related MSL stdio/wchar headers by removing duplicate FILE and enum definitions and updating fwide to use shared file_mode/orientation names. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 277-line asm implementation asm/MSL/strtoul.s, including __strtoul and the MSL_StrToUl jump table/data labels.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/MSL/strtoul.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so only src/MSL/strtoul.c.o supplies the symbol.
Added a 128-line C implementation of unsigned long __strtoul(int base, int max_width, int (*ReadProc)(void*, int, int), void* ReadProcArg, int* chars_scanned, int* negative, int* overflow).

The C __strtoul initializes negative and overflow, validates base and max_width, fetches input through the ReadProc-backed fetch macro/helper, then runs a scan_state switch until final_state(scan_state).

The scanner handles leading whitespace, optional '+'/'-' signs, base 0/8/10/16 behavior, 0x or 0X prefixes, decimal and alphabetic digits through isdigit/isalpha/toupper, ULONG_MAX overflow checks, chars_scanned adjustment, and unfetch(c) on exit.
Removed a duplicate FILE struct definition from src/stdio.h.
Removed console I/O prototypes for __close_console, __write_console, and __read_console from src/MSL/ansi_files.h.
Changed wchar_io.h to include ansi_files.h and removed local enum definitions for file kinds and file orientation.
Updated fwide in src/MSL/wchar_io.c from stream->mode and __unoriented/__wide_oriented/__char_oriented to stream->file_mode and UNORIENTED/WIDE_ORIENTED/CHAR_ORIENTED.
smart_moves: Modeled the old assembly jump-table behavior as an explicit scan_state switch, making the decompiled logic readable while preserving state-machine control flow needed for matching.

Kept the callback-based input interface intact: __strtoul consumes characters with fetch(), returns one with unfetch(c), and reports count/negative/overflow through out-parameters, matching the MSL scanning helper style.
Used ULONG_MAX / base as value_max and checked both pre-multiply and add overflow paths, mirroring the assembly's divwu/mullw/subfic overflow logic.
Removed the asm object from the build in the same PR as deleting the asm file, avoiding duplicate symbol or stale object issues.
Consolidated FILE/file-mode/orientation definitions instead of leaving parallel local copies in wchar_io.h and stdio.h, reducing struct-layout drift risk for later MSL work.
decomp_lessons: When replacing an asm scanner, preserve the original state machine structure first; readable state names like start, check_for_zero, need_digit, digit_loop, finished, and failure make the conversion easier to audit against branch labels.
For MSL numeric scanners, match edge cases around base validation: base < 0, base == 1, base > 36, and max_width < 1 all enter failure handling.
Base autodetection matters for matching and correctness: base 0 defaults to decimal unless a leading 0 makes octal, and a leading 0 followed by x/X sets base 16.
chars_scanned accounting can be non-obvious: this implementation increments through fetch, then decrements count on success and resets count/value on failure before storing *chars_scanned.
Header cleanup can be a prerequisite or side effect of matching: duplicate FILE and enum definitions can force wrong field names or layouts, as shown by fwide moving from mode to file_mode.
If a C file already has the public wrapper, adding the internal helper to the same file and removing the corresponding asm object is the clean path to retire an asm stub.
naming_conventions: __strtoul is kept as the internal MSL helper name, distinct from public strtoul.
ReadProc and ReadProcArg are capitalized in the parameter list, matching the callback naming visible in the added implementation.
The state variable is named scan_state, with descriptive state names such as start, check_for_zero, need_digit, digit_loop, finished, and failure where available.
Out-parameters are named chars_scanned, negative, and overflow, matching their semantic roles.

Shared FILE mode/orientation fields use file_mode and enum constants UNORIENTED, WIDE_ORIENTED, and CHAR_ORIENTED; older local names like __unoriented and __wide_oriented were removed from wchar_io.h.
The old assembly labels used generated names like MSL_StrToUl_80325C98 and MSL_StrToUl_80400728; the C replacement avoids carrying those label names forward.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the assembly jump table MSL_StrToUl_80400728 into a C switch over scan_state.
Matched the assembly's callback call pattern with fetch() and unfetch(c) abstractions rather than direct string indexing.
Preserved the loop guard shape: count <= max_width, c != -1, and !final_state(scan_state).
Used ctype helpers isspace, isdigit, isalpha, and toupper to represent the assembly's __ctype_map tests and toupper calls.
Replicated overflow detection using value_max = ULONG_MAX / base, a pre-multiply value > value_max check, and an add check against ULONG_MAX - value.

The added switch includes a raw case 4 while nearby states are named; this is likely evidence of preserving an exact state value/jump-table index for matching, though the slice does not include comments explaining it.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on title, changed files, and diff evidence only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/MSL/strtoul.c to see whether the raw case 4 state was renamed or further clarified.
Search for other MSL scanner helpers using ReadProc/fetch/unfetch for reusable matching patterns.
Search for PRs that moved FILE or __file_modes definitions into common_structs.h or ansi_files.h to understand the header consolidation sequence.
Check whether console_io prototypes removed from ansi_files.h were moved to a dedicated header in nearby PRs.
